lyrics:
Change is hard. We've all been told
Growth begins when comfort folds
The posters say, "Just Dare To Try"
And, "Pain is just a better why"
They take your desk, they change your name
Then smile and say, "It's not a game."

This isn't growth. It's just pretend-
A brand-new cut with the same end

All change is painful. All pain is earned
What you resist is what you've not learned
We burn the bridge, then light your way
If you feel lost, that's how you stay
Adapt. Align. Absorb. Obey
You'll thank us all someday

They said, "It's hard. But that means true."
We nodded, hoped that it was true
We bent, we strained, we took the heat
We lost our names, but stayed on beat
But change that leads to something real
Doesn't look like this. Don't make you kneel

This isn't growth. It's just pretend-
A brand-new cut with the same end

All change is painful. All pain is earned
What you resist is what you've not learned
We burn the bridge, then light your way
If you feel lost, that's how you stay
Adapt. Align. Absorb. Obey
You'll thank us all someday

But how does this make things better

That question is below the line
Please review Module 3: Our Attitude Pillar™

Welcome to the Attitude Pillar
Where resilience meets opportunity\
Remember: change isn't about comfort
It's about commitment
Growth is painful
because you're growing

All change is painful. All pain is right
Trust the script, ignore the sight
We bend the truth to shape the frame
And call it culture. Call it flame
The old ways die. We dim the past
We move too fast to see who lasts
This is the cost to feel renewed
This is the cut we call success

If it isn't broke
persona:
## 4Castle

The Future of Rock, The Power of Harmony

**4Castle **is a genre-defying, five-piece supergroup under Maricopa Records, built on a foundation of powerful vocal harmonies, intricate musicianship, and fearless innovation. Rooted in rock, but unbound by limitations, their music fuses elements of progressive rock, funk, Latin rhythms, blues, and folk, delivering a sound that is both technically complex and emotionally resonant.

### **A Band Like No Other**

Each member of **4Castle **brings a distinct voice-both musically and vocally-to the mix:
🎸 **Raven Cortez**(Lead Guitar, Vocals) – A fiery virtuoso with Latin and blues influences, weaving face-melting solos into every performance.
🎸 **Skylar “Sky” Monroe**(Rhythm Guitar, Vocals) – The poetic soul of the band, crafting melodic foundations and lyrically rich storytelling.
🎹 **Jonah Vega**(Keyboards, Vocals) – The sonic architect, layering psychedelic synths, Hammond organ swells, and cinematic orchestrations.
🎸 **Malcolm “Mack” Bishop**(Bass, Vocals) – The groove master, blending funk, jazz, and progressive rock into fluid, deep-pocket basslines.
🥁 **Eli “Fox” Foster **(Drums, Vocals) – The wild heartbeat, pushing the band forward with thunderous fills, polyrhythmic grooves, and explosive energy.

### **What Defines 4Castle?**

- **Multi-Layered Vocal Harmonies**– All five members **sing**, creating **rich, choral-like textures**that elevate every song.

- **Complex Arrangements & Bold Songwriting**– Each track is an **immersive journey**, balancing **technical mastery with raw emotion**.

- **Rhythmic Power & Innovation**– From **tight funk grooves to shifting time signatures**, their music **moves you-literally and figuratively**.

- **Lyrically Challenging Yet Accessible**– Their songs explore **deep themes of life, change, and resilience**, wrapped in **memorable hooks and melodies**.

### **4Castle’s Sound**

Imagine putting Emerson, Lake & Palmer, Heart, Led Zeppelin, Sly Stone, and Carlos Santana in a blender. The result? **4Castle**. Their music pays homage to the greats while pushing rock’s boundaries into the future.

### **Join the Journey**

With Maricopa Records, **4Castle **is set to redefine what a modern rock band can be. Expect genre-bending albums, electrifying performances, and music that resonates across generations.

🚀 Follow **4Castle **as they build their legacy-one song, one harmony, and one unforgettable moment at a time.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/4Cmxf7xuTHzf7UahMK2xJh)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/4castle/1801052673)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/5-rooks-in-a-dark-setting-1.png)
## Releases

impression.text: The track opens with a tense, atmospheric drone and melancholic vocals, establishing a dark and introspective mood that feels both cinematic and emotionally heavy. This minimalist beginning quickly erupts into a high-energy synth-rock anthem, driven by aggressive power-chord synths, driving percussion, and soaring, dramatic vocals. The production is expertly polished, creating a vast sense of space where crisp, forward vocals sit atop dense layers of distorted synth textures and punchy drums without muddying the mix. A notable spoken-word vocal bridge adds a robotic, narrative element before launching into the track's final explosive climax.

review.review_text: This is 4Castle's sharpest piece of satire yet, a takedown of corporate change-speak that lands because it commits to the bit. The track opens tense and brooding in D major before it snaps into a 123-BPM power-chord drive, and the genius is in the lyric: "Adapt. Align. Absorb. Obey / You'll thank us all someday" is the kind of line that sticks in your teeth. That spoken-word interlude welcoming you to the "Attitude Pillar" could have felt gimmicky, but it sells the dystopian HR-handbook tone instead, and the cold cut to "If it isn't broke" at the end is a perfect mic-drop. A highlight, and proof these guys can write with a knife.
anchors_used: Adapt. Align. Absorb. Obey / You'll thank us all someday
tense brooding open into power-chord drive
